title: Cards Game Rules
entries: {pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} plays a card from his or her hand face up or face down on the table. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} draws one or more cards from a personal deck. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} draws one card from a community deck. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} draws a card from a community deck if he or she has no other plays. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} places a card from his or her hand into the discard pile. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} lays down a pair, a three or four of a kind, or a straight on the table. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.

{pick:The rules are simple, on each turn the player|Play is quick — each turn, a player|The rules are simple: on their turn, a player} lays down a card to build threes and fours of a kind or straights on the table. {pick:To win, they must|To take the game, they must|To claim victory, one must} {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-winner} The best {pick:card|hand} is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-best}, and the worst is called {table:gm/tavern/game-cards-worst}.
